title: Shared Memory, Mutexes, Atomics, the Memory Model, and Race Evidence · Execution Trace: Race Detector Limits
summary: Concurrent workers update shared counters, caches, and snapshots; plausible output hides races and broken publication. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go race detector limits increment in a race-free shared-state component. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Run representative workloads with the race detector, interpret reports, retain reproductions, and state unexecuted-path limits.
Use typed atomics for isolated counters or immutable snapshot publication and reject multi-variable invariants without coordination.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

theory case — Workers concurrently publish a map-backed snapshot and increment two related counters. Output looks plausible in 4 runs. Establish happens-before edges, choose channel, mutex, or atomic ownership, and state what go test -race can and cannot prove.
